item.title: PE Firm Lightrock Raises $500 Million for New Clean Energy Fund
item.description: Private equity firm Lightrock, which was spun out from Liechtenstein’s LGT Group, has raised a new $500 million fund to back businesses advancing access to affordable and clean energy in Asia and Africa.

item.title: Fed’s Barr Says Shrinking Balance Sheet is ‘Wrong Objective’
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr on Thursday pushed back against proposals to shrink the central bank’s balance sheet, describing them as wrong and a threat to financial stability.

item.title: Gold Heads for Weekly Drop as Inflation Fuels Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: Gold headed for a weekly decline as a war-driven surge in US inflation fuels expectations for higher interest rates.

item.title: Rally in Top Space Stocks Sets Short Sellers Up for Squeeze
item.description: Two top performing space stocks have been on blistering rallies that risk soon burning short sellers betting against the highfliers, according to data provider S3 Partners.

item.title: Apollo’s West Technology Is in Talks to Sell Remaining Business
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc.-backed West Technology is engaged in exclusive talks to sell its remaining operating business to a counter-party, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Musk, Altman Make Final Pitches to Jury in Battle Over OpenAI
item.description: A jury will soon cast a vote and a judge will ultimately decide the outcome of a high-stakes showdown between Elon Musk and Sam Altman over OpenAI, the artificial intelligence startup they founded together more than a decade ago.

item.title: Oil Heads for Weekly Advance With Iran War Resolution at Impasse
item.description: Oil headed for a weekly gain as the crucial Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed with efforts to end the war in limbo, prolonging disruptions that have upended global markets.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Rise as AI Rally Spurs Wall Street: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were set to rise Friday after Wall Street hit fresh records, buoyed by a rally in artificial intelligence shares, strong corporate earnings and further signs of resilience in the US consumer.

item.title: Foreign Investors Fear Retreat in Japan’s Governance Reforms
item.description: Investor concern is mounting over a possible rollback of Japan’s corporate governance reforms, a key driver of foreign investment, threatening a rally that pushed equities to record levels.

item.title: Winklevosses Invest $100 Million in Battered Gemini Exchange
item.description: Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss, the billionaire twins who founded Gemini Space Station Inc., made a $100 million “strategic investment” into the money-losing crypto exchange.

item.title: World Bank Team to Visit Venezuela to Meet Government Officials
item.description: A group of representatives from the World Bank will visit Caracas to engage with government authorities for the first time since reestablishing formal relations last month, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chicago Fed Names Gadi Barlevy Director of Research Division
item.description: Gadi Barlevy was selected to lead the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago’s research division, the bank said in a statement Thursday.

item.title: Why Rare Earths Are China’s Trump Card in Trade War
item.description: Rare earths are among the most critical raw materials on the planet, deeply embedded in the technologies that underpin modern life — from iPhones and cars to data centers and MRI machines.

item.title: Blackstone, KKR to Take Over Dental Firm After Slashing 70% Debt
item.description: Direct lenders including Blackstone Inc. and KKR & Co. are set to take control of struggling dental business Affordable Care and slash about 70% of the debt as part of a restructuring agreement, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Red Hot Demand for Risky US Dollar Loans Brings Bigger Deals
item.description: Firms are borrowing more than expected in the US leveraged loan market as they take advantage of strong investor appetite for riskier debt.

item.title: AI Rally Crushes Stock Pickers With Just 1 in 4 Beating Market
item.description: Active managers who briefly looked like they might finally have their moment earlier this year are once again confronting a familiar problem: a market rally driven by a tiny group of tech megacaps that diversified portfolios simply can’t keep up with.

item.title: Musk’s xAI Unveils First Coding Agent in Bid to Rival Anthropic
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI is rolling out its first artificial intelligence coding agent, called Grok Build, in an attempt to catch up to Anthropic PBC’s Claude on streamlining software development.

item.title: JPMorgan Portfolio Manager Sees AI, Retail Risks for High-Grade Debt Rally
item.description: JPMorgan Asset Management’s bullish view on the high-grade corporate bond rally faces two potential stumbling blocks: an even bigger surge in technology spending and waning retail demand.

item.title: Figma Sees Strong Sales Growth, Touts New AI Monetization
item.description: Figma Inc. gave an outlook for revenue in the current period that topped analysts’ estimates, touting early traction from charging users directly for AI features.

item.title: Applied Materials’ Sales Forecast Gets Boost From AI Demand
item.description: Applied Materials Inc., the largest US supplier of semiconductor equipment, gave sales and profit forecasts that far exceeded analysts’ estimates, fueled by soaring demand for AI computing and memory chips.

item.title: Kushner’s Thrive Capital Invests $100 Million in Shopify
item.description: Joshua Kushner’s Thrive Capital has taken a stake in Shopify Inc., according to people familiar with the matter, marking a rare bet by the venture firm on a public company.

item.title: Apollo’s Insurance Unit Is FHLB System’s Third-Biggest Borrower
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc.’s insurance arm was the third-largest borrower at the end of March in the Federal Home Loan Bank system, further increasing its holdings since the end of last year.

item.title: We’re Going to Talk About the Future of Trading at Odd Lots Live
item.description: Come see us on May 28 in NYC.

item.title: Newsom Pitches Software Tax to Raise Billions in New Revenue
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom is proposing a new tax on cloud-based software sales to raise billions of dollars in state and local revenue in coming years.

item.title: Cerebras CEO Turns Year’s Largest IPO Into $3.2 Billion Fortune
item.description: Serial Silicon Valley entrepreneur Andrew Feldman, who literally grew up on the campus of Stanford University, was already involved in selling three companies and taking another public. But none of that compares with Thursday’s debut of his AI-chip manufacturer.

item.title: Argentina Inflation Slowed in April for First Time in 11 Months
item.description: Argentina’s inflation slowed for the first time in 11 months in a victory for President Javier Milei after prices had jumped on the Iran war-related oil shock in March.

item.title: Schonfeld-Backed Perbak Closing Hedge Fund After ‘Too-Short’ Run
item.description: Perbak Capital Partners, a UK-based hedge fund backed by Schonfeld Strategic Advisors, is winding down after determining its assets haven’t grown enough to maintain a “significant financial buffer” to protect the business.

item.title: Foreign Buyers Snap Up Nearly Half of Trump’s Emergency Oil
item.description: Nearly half of the crude oil released from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve is being exported, a fresh sign of how severely global supplies have tightened amid the Iran war.

item.title: Benchmark, Eclipse Reap Billions in Returns From Cerebras IPO
item.description: Three of the first venture capital investors in Cerebras Systems Inc. are set to earn billions of dollars from their early bets on the chipmaker following its Wall Street debut on Thursday. Benchmark will own roughly 8.1% of the outstanding shares in Cerebras after the initial public offering, according to a securities filing. At the $185-per-share IPO price for Cerebras, that stake was worth about $3.2 billion. That marks a 12-fold return for Benchmark, which invested a total of $268 million in

item.title: Brazilian Oil Jobs Are at 16-Year High on Offshore Drilling Boom
item.description: Employment in the Brazilian oil industry has rebounded to the highest since the golden era of Petrobras’s mega-projects in 2010.
